title: Ensure that the controller-manager.conf file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the controller-manager.conf file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the controller-manager.conf file has permissions of 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The controller-manager.conf file is the kubeconfig file for the Controller Manager. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the following command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf Verify that the permissions are 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chmod 600 /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf
input_row.default_value: By default, controller-manager.conf has permissions of 640 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the Control Plane node where the `controller-manager.conf` file is located.
2. Run the command: `stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf`
3. Verify that the output shows permissions of `600` or more restrictive (e.g., `600`, `400`).
4. If the permissions are not `600` or more restrictive, run the command: `chmod 600 /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf` to set the correct permissions.
